# claude-swarm — Complete Reference

> CLI for parallel Claude Code sessions to coordinate via SQLite.

claude-swarm lets multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code sessions) working on the same codebase stay in sync, communicate, claim files, review each other's work, and push safely through a shared git lock. All state lives in a single SQLite database. No external services, no Redis, no message queues.


## Install

    pip install claude-swarm

    # or via Homebrew
    brew tap laurenalexander2/tap && brew install claude-swarm

Requires Python 3.11+. Dependencies: click >= 8.0, rich >= 13.0.


## Quick Start

    ao start "Build a REST API with auth"       # launch orchestrator mode

What ao start does:
1. Checks Claude Code CLI is installed
2. Creates .claude-swarm/ directory
3. Writes/appends CLAUDE.md with full coordination protocol
4. Launches Claude Code with an orchestrator prompt
5. The orchestrator decomposes the project into workstreams
6. Prints copy-pasteable prompts for each session (one per terminal)
7. You paste each prompt into a separate Claude Code terminal
8. The orchestrator monitors all sessions via the SQLite bus

Manual setup alternative:

    claude-swarm init --sessions "A:frontend B:backend C:infra"
    claude-swarm status


## Workflow (Recommended Process)

1. Pull before starting
2. Sync between every task (claude-swarm sync --session ID)
3. Share decisions via context add whenever you make a choice that affects others
4. Claim files before editing to prevent concurrent edits
5. Update status when starting, blocking, or completing work
6. Commit often with small, focused commits
7. Request review when ready to merge
8. Wait for merge-ok before pushing
9. Push only via the CLI (never raw git push) — respects the lock
10. Never force push


## Global Options

    claude-swarm --db PATH COMMAND
    export CLAUDE_SWARM_DB=".claude-swarm/bus.db"
    claude-swarm --version


## Commands


### claude-swarm sync --session SESSION_ID

The most important command. Run between every task. Does three things in one call:
1. Checks inbox (returns new messages, marks them read)
2. Reads shared context (only entries since last sync)
3. Sends heartbeat (so orchestrator knows the session is alive)

Options:
    --session  (required)  Session ID to sync

Output when nothing new: silent (no output, exit 0)
Output when something new:
    [SYNC] 1 new message(s), 2 new context
    MSG from=B: "Auth types are exported now"
    CTX [decision] by=B: "JWT for auth, not session cookies"
    CTX [interface] by=C: "POST /users returns {id, email, created_at}"


### claude-swarm context add BODY --session SESSION_ID --category CATEGORY

Add a shared context entry. Categories: decision, interface, warning, convention, discovery.

Options:
    --session   (required)  Session ID
    --category  (required)  One of: decision, interface, warning, convention, discovery

Examples:
    claude-swarm context add "API uses snake_case for all fields" --session A --category decision
    claude-swarm context add "POST /users returns {id, email, created_at}" --session B --category interface
    claude-swarm context add "Don't touch src/db.py — migration in progress" --session C --category warning


### claude-swarm context show

Show all shared context entries. Prints table: ID, Session, Category, Body, Created.


### claude-swarm message TO_ID BODY --from FROM_ID

Send a message to another session.

Arguments:
    TO_ID   Recipient session ID
    BODY    Message text
Options:
    --from  (required)  Sender session ID

Example:
    claude-swarm message B "Can you expose the auth types?" --from A


### claude-swarm inbox --session SESSION_ID

Check unread messages and pending reviews for a session.

Options:
    --session  (required)  Session ID

Shows unread messages (table: ID, From, Body, Type, Time) and pending reviews assigned to this session.


### claude-swarm reply MESSAGE_ID BODY --from FROM_ID

Reply to a message. Marks original as read, sends reply to original sender.

Arguments:
    MESSAGE_ID  ID of message to reply to (integer)
    BODY        Reply text
Options:
    --from  (required)  Sender session ID

Example:
    claude-swarm reply 1 "Done, exported from auth/types.ts" --from B


### claude-swarm init --sessions "A:frontend B:backend C:infra"

Initialize the database and register sessions.

Options:
    --sessions  (required)  Space-separated "ID:workstream" pairs

Each session gets status "running" and branch "feat/{workstream}".


### claude-swarm status

Show all sessions. Prints table: ID, Workstream, Status, Branch, Note, Updated.


### claude-swarm update SESSION_ID --status STATUS --note NOTE

Update a session's status or note.

Arguments:
    SESSION_ID  Session to update
Options:
    --status  New status: running, blocked, done
    --note    Status note

Examples:
    claude-swarm update A --status blocked --note "waiting on B for types"
    claude-swarm update A --status running --note "implementing rate limiter"


### claude-swarm claim FILE_PATH --session SESSION_ID

Claim a file for exclusive editing.

Arguments:
    FILE_PATH  Path to file
Options:
    --session  (required)  Session ID

Exit 0 on success. Exit 1 if already claimed by another session.

Example:
    claude-swarm claim src/auth/middleware.py --session A


### claude-swarm claims

List all active file claims. Prints table: File, Session, Claimed At.


### claude-swarm unclaim FILE_PATH --session SESSION_ID

Release a file claim.

Arguments:
    FILE_PATH  Path to file
Options:
    --session  (required)  Session ID

Example:
    claude-swarm unclaim src/auth/middleware.py --session A


### claude-swarm review request --from FROM_ID --to TO_ID --diff DIFF_TEXT

Request a review from another session.

Options:
    --from  (required)  Requester session ID
    --to    (required)  Reviewer session ID
    --diff  (required)  Diff content

Example:
    claude-swarm review request --from A --to B --diff "$(git diff main)"


### claude-swarm review list

List all reviews. Prints table: ID, Requester, Reviewer, Status, Comments, Created.


### claude-swarm review show REVIEW_ID

Show details of a review: Requester, Reviewer, Status, Comments, full Diff.

Arguments:
    REVIEW_ID  Review ID (integer)


### claude-swarm review approve REVIEW_ID --from FROM_ID --comment COMMENT

Approve a review.

Arguments:
    REVIEW_ID  Review ID (integer)
Options:
    --from     (required)  Reviewer session ID
    --comment  (optional, default "")  Approval comment

Sends approval notification to requester. Requester can now pass merge-ok.


### claude-swarm review reject REVIEW_ID --from FROM_ID --comment COMMENT

Reject a review.

Arguments:
    REVIEW_ID  Review ID (integer)
Options:
    --from     (required)  Reviewer session ID
    --comment  (optional, default "")  Rejection reason

Resets status to pending. Sends rejection notification to requester.


### claude-swarm merge-ok SESSION_ID

Check if a session is clear to merge.

Arguments:
    SESSION_ID  Session to check

Exit 0 if clear (all reviews approved or no reviews). Exit 1 if any pending reviews.


### claude-swarm commit DESCRIPTION --session SESSION_ID

Commit staged changes with session-prefixed message.

Arguments:
    DESCRIPTION  Commit message
Options:
    --session  (required)  Session ID

Message format: "[Session A] add PII detection middleware"


### claude-swarm push --session SESSION_ID

Push changes. Acquires git lock, rebases, pushes, releases lock.

Options:
    --session  (required)  Session ID

On failure: sets session status to "blocked" with note "push failed".


### claude-swarm pull --session SESSION_ID

Pull and rebase on latest.

Options:
    --session  (required)  Session ID


### claude-swarm git-status

Show git status.


### claude-swarm git-diff --staged

Show git diff.

Options:
    --staged  (flag)  Show only staged changes


### claude-swarm git-log

Show recent git log.


### claude-swarm orchestrate run

Start the orchestrator poll loop.

    claude-swarm orchestrate run --interval 10 --auto-approve --stale-minutes 15 --quiet

Options:
    --interval       (default 10)   Poll interval in seconds
    --auto-approve   (flag)         Auto-approve reviews assigned to orchestrator
    --stale-minutes  (default 15)   Minutes before session is considered stale
    --quiet          (flag)         Only print when there are events

Detects blocked sessions, stale sessions, pending reviews, unread messages.


### claude-swarm orchestrate dashboard

One-shot status view: sessions, messages, reviews, file claims.


### ao setup

Check that Claude Code CLI is installed. Exit 0 if found, exit 1 if not.


### ao start DESCRIPTION --project-dir DIR

Launch orchestrator mode.

Arguments:
    DESCRIPTION  Project description
Options:
    --project-dir  (default ".")  Project directory


## Architecture

    claude_swarm/
    ├── bus.py          # SQLite message bus (sessions, messages, reviews, claims, context, sync)
    ├── git.py          # Git operations + push lock management
    ├── merge.py        # Conflict self-healing + file owner notifications
    ├── orchestrator.py # Poll loop, blocked/stale detection, auto-approve
    ├── cli.py          # Click CLI commands (thin layer over other modules)
    └── ao.py           # Quick-start entry point (ao setup, ao start)


## Database Schema (6 tables)


### sessions

    CREATE TABLE sessions (
        id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
        workstream  TEXT NOT NULL,
        status      TEXT DEFAULT 'running',
        branch      TEXT,
        note        TEXT,
        updated_at  DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
        last_sync_at DATETIME
    );

Fields: id (e.g. "A"), workstream (e.g. "frontend"), status (running/blocked/done), branch (e.g. "feat/frontend"), note (blocker reason), updated_at (heartbeat), last_sync_at (for context filtering).


### messages

    CREATE TABLE messages (
        id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
        from_id     TEXT NOT NULL,
        to_id       TEXT NOT NULL,
        body        TEXT NOT NULL,
        type        TEXT DEFAULT 'message',
        status      TEXT DEFAULT 'unread',
        created_at  DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    );

Types: "message", "review_request", "review_approved", "review_rejected". Status: "unread", "read".


### reviews

    CREATE TABLE reviews (
        id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
        requester   TEXT NOT NULL,
        reviewer    TEXT NOT NULL,
        diff        TEXT,
        status      TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',
        comments    TEXT,
        created_at  DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    );

Status: "pending", "approved". Rejection resets to "pending". merge-ok checks for zero non-approved reviews.


### git_lock

    CREATE TABLE git_lock (
        id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1),
        held_by     TEXT,
        acquired_at DATETIME
    );

Single-row table. held_by is session ID or NULL. Serializes pushes to prevent rebase collisions.


### file_claims

    CREATE TABLE file_claims (
        file_path   TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
        session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,
        claimed_at  DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    );

One owner per file. claim fails if claimed by different session. Same session can re-claim.


### shared_context

    CREATE TABLE shared_context (
        id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
        session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,
        category    TEXT NOT NULL,
        body        TEXT NOT NULL,
        created_at  DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    );

Categories: "decision", "interface", "warning", "convention", "discovery". Timestamps use Python microsecond precision for sync filtering.


## Testing

90 tests. Run with:

    pytest tests/ -v


## Source

- GitHub: https://github.com/laurenalexander2/agent-orchestrator
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/claude-swarm/
